segments.text: Okay, so today we are going to have the third lecture where we will continue from the things
that we had seen before.
So let me first recall the stuff that we had seen last time.
We had talked about two things namely the row echelon form and the reduced row echelon
form.
We saw things called row operations.
There were three types of row operations which we call as elementary row operations.
So we had three of them.
One was multiplying the row by something, the other one was swapping the rows and the
third one was trying to add a row, a multiple of one row to the other.
So I will just write them in a nutshell so that we are reminded.
Third thing we had done was adding a multiple of one row to the other.
So using these row operations we claim that we can get a row echelon form of any matrix
and also the reduced row echelon form of that matrix.
Now one thing which I would like to remind and if we see in the previous example that
we did last time, the row echelon form is not unique.
In fact a matrix can have infinitely many row echelon forms but when it comes to
a unique form.
So in some sense the importance of reduced row echelon form also lies in the fact that
it is the only form for a given matrix.
So that anybody who does reduced row echelon form should end up getting the same matrix.
One more thing I had done last time, so let me recall that for you, I used this
abbreviation RREF to refer to the reduced row echelon form.
Okay so I pick up the example that we had seen last time, we were trying to deal with
this system of equations x plus 3y equal to 4, 5x minus 4y is minus 7 and we wanted
to find the solution using the ideas of echelon forms.
So we said let us create the augmented matrix 1, 5, 3, minus 4, minus 7 and we will
try to get its echelon form and we will try to get the reduced row echelon form as well.
So the idea of echelon form and reduced row echelon form was that we wanted to produce,
let us say we want the reduced row echelon form, we want to produce 1s as many 1s
we can produce in a column or it may happen something like this, 1, 0, 0, 0, 1 in
which case this should become 0.
So one of these guys is what we are aiming for and as you can see both of them are reduced
row echelon form or possible row reduced echelon forms of this matrix.
It may happen that we end up with this, it may happen that we end up with this
and based on what we end up with, we can tell you something about the solution
for this system of equations.
Okay so let us try and see how things go.
Since we want to create 1s and 0s, so we will try to use row operations.
We already have one over here so that is nice.
We use this entry to try to take this entry out and to do that we will use a row operation
which says minus 5 times row 1 added to row 2.
Now remember whichever row we are using as a multiplier row will remain unchanged
and the action is done on the row which is our target.
So this would be 0, this would be minus 15, minus 4 gives you minus 19
and this is minus 20 and minus 7 so this should give you minus 27.
Now let us ask ourselves a question.
Is this an echelon form for my matrix which is written over here?
And the answer to that is yes, if you recall echelon forms,
all 0 rows should be at the bottom, we do not have a 0 row
and all the leading entries or leading non-zero entries should have 0s underneath them.
So this is a leading entry, this is a leading entry
and as you can see they do satisfy the definition of echelon form.
So this is a possible echelon form.
Let us see if we can get something out of it.
Of course we can solve the system from here itself but we will try to do
and see if we can go for the row reduced echelon form.
Now let us say if I want to go for the row reduced echelon form,
remember this is echelon.
If I want to go for the row reduced echelon form,
I need to make this guy 1.
So for that I will use another row operation.
I will multiply the entire second row by minus 1 over 19 so that I can get this as 1.
So this is 1, 3, 4, 0, 1, 27 over 19 and this is what we are getting.
Now if you notice we have got this new form which is again an echelon form.
So it kind of corroborates what we had mentioned over here
that the echelon form need not be unique.
Right now we have two echelon forms.
Both of them are good, equally good.
But suppose I want to go from here to the row reduced echelon form,
then I should use this entry to try to make this entry the one above and 0.
Because in the row reduced echelon form,
the leading non-zero entry should be the only non-zero entry in its column.
So if I want to do that, I will say minus 3 times row 2 added to row 1.
And let us see what the effect of that is.
So this will give us 1, but let us first write this guy out.
So minus 3 times 0 added to 1 will give you 1.
Minus 3 plus 3 is 0.
And minus 3 when added to, when multiplied to this and added to 4,
let us see, it will give you minus 81 over 19 plus 4.
So it will give you minus 5 over 19.
If we come here and we say this is a row reduced echelon form,
it is indeed a row reduced echelon form because each of the leading entry,
each of the leading non-zero entry in its row has become 1.
And that is the only non-zero entry in that particular column.
So these are our leading entries here.
These are the leading entries over here.
These are the leading entries over here.
So leading entries will play a significant role later on when we talk more about these
kind of systems.
But right now let us see what have we achieved.
We say that this row reduced echelon form will help us
find the solutions to this system fairly easily.
And let us see the reason why.
Because if you look at it, if I convert it into the system of equations,
so I am going to convert the system again.
How does the system look like?
This will be x times 0 plus 0y equal to minus 5 over 19.
So if I ignore the 0y, so can I say x is minus 5 over 19.
And likewise in the second column, y will be 27 over 19.
So if you look at the row reduced echelon form,
it pretty much gives me the solution right away without doing anything else.
I can simply read out the solutions using this form.
And that is the convenience we are after that given any system,
can I somehow do reduce it to its row reduced echelon form.
And once I have that, I can talk about its solutions.
Now let me introduce a terminology which I had mentioned last time also.
Let us talk about two things.
One of them is the idea of a pivot entry.
And then we will talk about non-pivot entries.
Of course, anything which is not pivot will qualify as non-pivot.
So non-pivot is not something very significant for us
in terms of non-pivot entries.
But what is a pivot entry?
Pivot entry for us would be the same as the leading non-zero entry.
As we shall see that it will correspond to the leading non-zero entries
of the row reduced echelon forms also.
So for example, these guys or these guys or these guys are all pivots.
We would be interested in knowing the pivot entries
for a reason that will be clear a little later.
Now since while we are at it, let us see what non-pivots are.
Of course, any entry which is not a pivot entry is a non-pivot entry.
Nothing of significance there.
But we would be interested in something called a pivot column.
Sometimes they are also referred to as the basic columns.
So pivot columns will be the ones which contain the pivot entry.
So we will say columns.
And just like pivot columns, we will also talk about things called free columns.
The columns do not have a pivot entry.
Okay, so let us first see how is this going to be applied in our present example.
The first column out here will be a pivot column because it has a pivot entry.
Likewise, the second column is also a pivot column.
And this column which does not have a pivot will be called the free column.
It is just that even though these definitions are fairly obvious,
it will just help us say things in a better way.
And that is why we are having these kind of names given to certain things.
Now let me also introduce two other things.

we will talk about two types of variables, pivot variables and free variables.
What would be pivot variables?

What I am going to say is as follows.

then that will give me the total number of variables.
It is a very obvious statement, but later on you will see it will be linked to one of the
most important theorems of linear algebra.
